```
diff --git a/app.js b/app.js
index c553882..c8c48a7 100644
--- a/app.js
+++ b/app.js
@@ -20,6 +20,69 @@ const supabaseClient = window.supabase.createClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_
 let pets = [];
 let realtimeChannel = null;
 
+const IMAGE_PLACEHOLDER_URL = 'https://via.placeholder.com/300x200?text=Sem+Foto';
+const IMAGE_TRANSFORM_WIDTH = 400;
+const IMAGE_TRANSFORM_QUALITY = 75;
+// Proxy opcional para CDN intermediario (ativo automaticamente no dominio netlify.app)
+const IMAGE_PROXY_BASE_URL = window.location.hostname.endsWith('.netlify.app') ? '/supabase-img' : '';
+const TRACKED_STATUSES = new Set(['DISPONIVEL', 'EM_PROCESSO_ADOTIVO', 'ADOTADO']);
+
+function applyImageProxy(url) {
+    if (!IMAGE_PROXY_BASE_URL) return url;
+
+    try {
+        const parsed = new URL(url);
+        return &#96;${IMAGE_PROXY_BASE_URL}${parsed.pathname}${parsed.search}&#96;;
+    } catch (_error) {
+        return url;
+    }
+}
+
+function buildCardImageUrl(originalUrl) {
+    if (!originalUrl) return IMAGE_PLACEHOLDER_URL;
+
+    try {
+        const parsed = new URL(originalUrl);
+        const publicPath = '/storage/v1/object/public/';
+        const renderPath = '/storage/v1/render/image/public/';
+
+        if (parsed.pathname.includes(publicPath)) {
+            parsed.pathname = parsed.pathname.replace(publicPath, renderPath);
+            parsed.searchParams.set('width', String(IMAGE_TRANSFORM_WIDTH));
+            parsed.searchParams.set('quality', String(IMAGE_TRANSFORM_QUALITY));
+        }
+
+        return applyImageProxy(parsed.toString());
+    } catch (_error) {
+        return applyImageProxy(originalUrl);
+    }
+}
+
+function getPetPhotos(pet) {
+    if (!Array.isArray(pet?.fotos)) return [];
+    return pet.fotos.filter((url) =&gt; typeof url === 'string' &amp;&amp; url.trim() !== '');
+}
+
+function getPetRenderSignature(pet) {
+    const photos = getPetPhotos(pet);
+    const caracteristicas = Array.isArray(pet.caracteristicas_especiais)
+        ? pet.caracteristicas_especiais.join('|')
+        : '';
+
+    return [
+        pet.id,
+        pet.nome,
+        pet.especie,
+        pet.idade,
+        pet.status,
+        pet.porte,
+        pet.sexo,
+        pet.raca || '',
+        caracteristicas,
+        photos[0] || '',
+    ].join('::');
+}
+
 // Mapeamento de características especiais
 const CARACTERISTICAS_ESPECIAIS = {
     cegueira_total: 'Cegueira total',
@@ -54,10 +117,13 @@ function createPetCard(pet) {
     const card = document.createElement('div');
     card.className = 'pet-card';
     card.setAttribute('data-pet-id', pet.id);
+    card.dataset.renderSignature = getPetRenderSignature(pet);
     
-    const fotoUrl = pet.fotos &amp;&amp; pet.fotos.length &gt; 0 
-        ? pet.fotos[0] 
-        : 'https://via.placeholder.com/300x200?text=Sem+Foto';
+    const originalPhotos = getPetPhotos(pet);
+    const primaryPhotoOriginal = originalPhotos[0] || null;
+    const fotoUrl = primaryPhotoOriginal
+        ? buildCardImageUrl(primaryPhotoOriginal)
+        : IMAGE_PLACEHOLDER_URL;
     
     const especieIcon = pet.especie === 'CÃO' ? 'fa-dog' : 'fa-cat';
     const sexoIcon = pet.sexo === 'MACHO' ? 'fa-mars' : 'fa-venus';
@@ -81,7 +147,7 @@ function createPetCard(pet) {
     }
     
     card.innerHTML = &#96;
-        &lt;img src="${fotoUrl}" alt="${pet.nome}" class="pet-image" loading="lazy" data-photos='${JSON.stringify(pet.fotos || [])}' style="cursor: pointer;" draggable="false" oncontextmenu="return false;"&gt;
+        &lt;img src="${fotoUrl}" alt="${pet.nome}" class="pet-image" loading="lazy" data-photos='${JSON.stringify(originalPhotos)}' style="cursor: pointer;" draggable="false" oncontextmenu="return false;"&gt;
         &lt;div class="pet-content"&gt;
             &lt;div class="pet-header"&gt;
                 &lt;div class="pet-name-container"&gt;
@@ -126,54 +192,69 @@ function createPetCard(pet) {
     return card;
 }
 
+function renderPetsColumn(container, petsByColumn, emptyStateHTML) {
+    if (petsByColumn.length === 0) {
+        if (!container.querySelector('.empty-state') || container.children.length !== 1) {
+            container.innerHTML = emptyStateHTML;
+        }
+        return;
+    }
+
+    const existingCardsById = new Map();
+    container.querySelectorAll('.pet-card').forEach((card) =&gt; {
+        existingCardsById.set(card.getAttribute('data-pet-id'), card);
+    });
+
+    const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
+
+    petsByColumn.forEach((pet) =&gt; {
+        const petId = String(pet.id);
+        const existingCard = existingCardsById.get(petId);
+        const nextSignature = getPetRenderSignature(pet);
+
+        let cardNode = existingCard;
+        if (!cardNode || cardNode.dataset.renderSignature !== nextSignature) {
+            cardNode = createPetCard(pet);
+        }
+
+        fragment.appendChild(cardNode);
+    });
+
+    container.replaceChildren(fragment);
+}
+
 // Função para renderizar pets em suas respectivas colunas
 function renderPets() {
     const disponivelContainer = document.getElementById('disponivel-cards');
     const processoContainer = document.getElementById('processo-cards');
     const adotadoContainer = document.getElementById('adotado-cards');
-

-    processoContainer.innerHTML = '';

-

+    const adotados = pets.filter((p) =&gt; p.status === 'ADOTADO');
+
     // Atualizar badges de contagem
     document.getElementById('disponivel-count').textContent = disponiveis.length;
     document.getElementById('processo-count').textContent = emProcesso.length;
     document.getElementById('adotado-count').textContent = adotados.length;
-

-    } else {
-        emProcesso.forEach(pet =&gt; {
-            processoContainer.appendChild(createPetCard(pet));
-        });
-    }
-    
-    // Renderizar pets adotados (apenas do dia atual)
-    if (adotados.length === 0) {
-        adotadoContainer.innerHTML = '&lt;div class="empty-state"&gt;&lt;i class="fas fa-home"&gt;&lt;/i&gt;&lt;p&gt;Nenhum pet adotado hoje&lt;/p&gt;&lt;/div&gt;';
-    } else {
-        adotados.forEach(pet =&gt; {
-            adotadoContainer.appendChild(createPetCard(pet));
-        });
-    }
-    
+
+    renderPetsColumn(
+        disponivelContainer,
+        disponiveis,
+        '&lt;div class="empty-state"&gt;&lt;i class="fas fa-heart"&gt;&lt;/i&gt;&lt;p&gt;Nenhum pet disponível&lt;/p&gt;&lt;/div&gt;'
+    );

+    );
+    renderPetsColumn(
+        adotadoContainer,
+        adotados,
+        '&lt;div class="empty-state"&gt;&lt;i class="fas fa-home"&gt;&lt;/i&gt;&lt;p&gt;Nenhum pet adotado hoje&lt;/p&gt;&lt;/div&gt;'
+    );
+
     // Atualizar badges das abas mobile
     updateTabBadges();
 }
@@ -274,6 +355,74 @@ function animateCardMove(petId, newStatus) {
     }
 }
 
+function isTrackedStatus(status) {
+    return TRACKED_STATUSES.has(status);
+}
+
+function upsertPetInState(pet) {
+    if (!pet?.id) return false;
+
+    const index = pets.findIndex((item) =&gt; item.id === pet.id);
+    if (index === -1) {
+        pets.push(pet);
+        return true;
+    }
+
+    pets[index] = pet;
+    return true;
+}
+
+function removePetFromState(petId) {
+    if (!petId) return false;
+    const originalLength = pets.length;
+    pets = pets.filter((item) =&gt; item.id !== petId);
+    return pets.length !== originalLength;
+}
+
+function shouldReloadFromDatabase(payload) {
+    const newStatus = payload?.new?.status;
+    const oldStatus = payload?.old?.status;
+
+    // A coluna de adotados depende de join com adocoes e filtro por data;
+    // para manter consistencia, recarregamos apenas quando ADOTADO for afetado.
+    return newStatus === 'ADOTADO' || oldStatus === 'ADOTADO';
+}
+
+function applyRealtimePayload(payload) {
+    if (!payload?.eventType) {
+        loadPets();
+        return;
+    }
+
+    if (shouldReloadFromDatabase(payload)) {
+        loadPets();
+        return;
+    }
+
+    let hasChanges = false;
+
+    if (payload.eventType === 'INSERT') {
+        if (isTrackedStatus(payload.new?.status)) {
+            hasChanges = upsertPetInState(payload.new);
+        }
+    } else if (payload.eventType === 'UPDATE') {
+        if (isTrackedStatus(payload.new?.status)) {
+            hasChanges = upsertPetInState(payload.new);
+        } else {
+            hasChanges = removePetFromState(payload.old?.id || payload.new?.id);
+        }
+    } else if (payload.eventType === 'DELETE') {
+        hasChanges = removePetFromState(payload.old?.id);
+    } else {
+        loadPets();
+        return;
+    }
+
+    if (hasChanges) {
+        renderPets();
+    }
+}
+
 // Configurar Realtime para atualizações em tempo real
 function setupRealtimeSubscription() {
     if (realtimeChannel) {
@@ -292,10 +441,7 @@ function setupRealtimeSubscription() {
             },
             (payload) =&gt; {
                 console.log('Mudança detectada:', payload);
-                
-                // Quando houver mudança, recarregar tudo para garantir que apenas
-                // pets adotados hoje aparecem
-                loadPets();
+                applyRealtimePayload(payload);
             }
         )
         .subscribe((status) =&gt; {
@@ -424,9 +570,6 @@ async function init() {
     updateDateTime();
     setInterval(updateDateTime, 60 * 1000);
     
-    // Atualizar a cada 5 minutos como fallback
-    setInterval(loadPets, 5 * 60 * 1000);
-

     setupMobileTabs();
     setupBoardScrollSync();
diff --git a/netlify.toml b/netlify.toml
index 108febc..bc9fea5 100644
--- a/netlify.toml
+++ b/netlify.toml
@@ -8,6 +8,13 @@
   # Variáveis de ambiente (se necessário no futuro)
   NODE_VERSION = "18"
 
+[[redirects]]
+  # Proxy de imagens Supabase para aproveitar cache da CDN da Netlify
+  from = "/supabase-img/*"
+  to = "https://dwrtskjadoocdxojkrlu.supabase.co/:splat"
+  status = 200
+  force = true
+
 [[redirects]]
   # Redirecionar todas as rotas para index.html (SPA behavior)
   from = "/*"
@@ -42,3 +49,9 @@
   [headers.values]
     Cache-Control = "public, max-age=0, must-revalidate"
 
+[[headers]]
+  # Cache agressivo para imagens proxied do Supabase
+  for = "/supabase-img/*"
+  [headers.values]
+    Cache-Control = "public, max-age=2592000, stale-while-revalidate=86400"
+
warning: in the working copy of 'app.js', LF will be replaced by CRLF the next time Git touches it

```
</details>
</tool-use>

---

Implementei o plano completo e finalizei **todos os TODOs** sem editar o arquivo de plano.

### O que foi implementado

- **Thumbnails com Supabase Image Transformations** nos cards (mantendo qualidade e largura controladas).
- **Remoção do polling de 5 minutos** (`setInterval(loadPets, ...)`) para eliminar recargas redundantes.
- **Atualização incremental via Realtime**: agora o payload é processado localmente e só faz `loadPets()` quando necessário (casos envolvendo `ADOTADO`, por depender do join com `adocoes`).
- **Render otimizado** com diff por card (`renderSignature`), evitando recriar todos os cards a cada atualização.
- **Proxy/CDN intermediário** via Netlify:
  - rota `/supabase-img/*` para Supabase Storage;
  - cache agressivo em `/supabase-img/*`.
- **Modal em full-res preservado**: o card usa thumbnail transformada, mas o modal continua usando as URLs originais.

### Trechos principais

```23:58:app.js
const IMAGE_PLACEHOLDER_URL = 'https://via.placeholder.com/300x200?text=Sem+Foto';
const IMAGE_TRANSFORM_WIDTH = 400;
const IMAGE_TRANSFORM_QUALITY = 75;
// Proxy opcional para CDN intermediario (ativo em produção; desativado em localhost)
const IMAGE_PROXY_BASE_URL = ['localhost', '127.0.0.1'].includes(window.location.hostname) ? '' : '/supabase-img';

function buildCardImageUrl(originalUrl) {
    // ... troca object/public por render/image/public + width/quality
}
```

```195:224:app.js
function renderPetsColumn(container, petsByColumn, emptyStateHTML) {
    // reaproveita card existente quando assinatura não mudou
    // só recria card quando houve mudança real
    // evita reconstrução total do DOM
}
```

```391:445:app.js
function applyRealtimePayload(payload) {
    // INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE incrementais
    // fallback para loadPets() apenas quando status ADOTADO é afetado
}
```

```563:575:app.js
async function init() {
    await loadPets();
    setupRealtimeSubscription();
    updateDateTime();
    setInterval(updateDateTime, 60 * 1000);
    // polling de 5 min removido
}
```

```11:16:netlify.toml
[[redirects]]
  from = "/supabase-img/*"
  to = "https://dwrtskjadoocdxojkrlu.supabase.co/:splat"
  status = 200
  force = true
```

```52:56:netlify.toml
[[headers]]
  for = "/supabase-img/*"
  [headers.values]
    Cache-Control = "public, max-age=2592000, stale-while-revalidate=86400"
```

### Validação executada

- `npm run build` executado com sucesso (build/minificação OK).
- `ReadLints` nos arquivos alterados: **sem erros**.

### Arquivos alterados

- `app.js`
- `netlify.toml`

Se quiser, no próximo passo eu posso te passar um checklist de monitoramento no Supabase (Storage e Realtime) para medir a queda real de egress em 24h/72h após deploy.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I can see the issue. The `.pet-image` CSS has:
```css
.pet-image {
    width: 100%;
    height: auto;
    display: block;
}
```

This means the image will render at full width of the card with its natural aspect ratio. Since many pet photos are portrait-oriented (taller than wide), the images become very tall. 

Before the image transformation changes, the images were served at full resolution. Now with the `width=400` parameter from the Supabase Image Transformations, the images are still being rendered at `width: 100%` of the card but with their natural height. The issue is that portrait photos at 400px width can be quite tall.
</details></think>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have all the information needed. Let me also check if there's a `height` parameter in the Supabase Image Transformations that could be added.

The issue is clear:

1. **CSS**: `.pet-image` has `width: 100%; height: auto;` - this means images will display at full width of the card container while maintaining their natural aspect ratio.

2. **Image Transformations**: The `buildCardImageUrl` function only sets `width=400` and `quality=75` but no `height` parameter. This means the Supabase Image Transformation resizes images to 400px width while maintaining the original aspect ratio.


3. Portrait-oriented photos from phones are the culprit—when scaled to fit the card width in a 3-column grid, they stretch vertically and make cards tower across the viewport. I could fix this with CSS by adding a fixed height and `object-fit: cover` to crop images to a consistent size, or I could add a height parameter to the image transformation itself.

The most efficient approach would combine server-side cropping through the image transformation with CSS constraints, so images are already optimized before reaching the browser and bandwidth is saved.

I need to verify the exact parameter names in the Supabase documentation, but based on what I know, I should set both `height` and `resize=cover` in the transformation URL while also adding `object-fit: cover` with a fixed CSS height to ensure consistent card dimensions regardless of the original image aspect ratio.

Looking back at what changed, the CSS for `.pet-image` was never modified—only the transformation was added—so the original `height: auto` behavior is still there, which means images are now being constrained to 400px width but allowed to scale vertically based on their aspect ratio, causing the card height inconsistency the user mentioned.

The solution is to add a fixed height constraint both in the transformation parameters and in the CSS to prevent portrait images from making the cards excessively tall.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Let me look at the network requests to see the actual image dimensions being served. But first, let me also check if there's a git history to compare with the original state of the CSS. Actually, from the conversation summary, the CSS was NOT modified - only `app.js` and `netlify.toml` were changed. So the CSS `.pet-image` has always been `width: 100%; height: auto;`.

But the user says "As alterações implementadas descaracterizaram o tamanho dos cards dos pets" - meaning the changes decharacterized the cards. Let me think about what could have changed.

Before the changes: Images were loaded from `/storage/v1/object/public/...` at full resolution. The browser would download the full image (e.g., 2000x3000) but display it at the card width (say 300px) with `height: auto`, so it'd be 300x450.


After the changes: Images are loaded from `/storage/v1/render/image/public/...?width=400&quality=75`. This means the Supabase Image Transformation resizes them to 400px wide, maintaining aspect ratio. For a 2000x3000 original, this becomes 400x600. The browser then scales to card width (300px), resulting in 300x450 display. So the display dimensions should be identical! The images are scaled by the browser either way, and the aspect ratio is preserved.

Unless... the Image Transformation endpoint is serving images differently somehow? Or maybe there's a difference I'm not seeing. Wait - maybe the issue is NOT related to our changes at all. The user might be seeing this for the first time and attributing it to our changes. OR maybe the issue was always there but was masked before.

Actually, let me re-read the user's message: "As alterações implementadas descaracterizaram o tamanho dos cards dos pets. creio que seja as dimensões das fotos que estão sendo carregadas nos cards." The user believes the changes affected card sizes. Let me check if there's any difference in the image transformation endpoint behavior.

Actually, I just realized something - the Supabase Image Transformation endpoint `/render/image/public/` might not work the same way in localhost (without the proxy). In localhost, `IMAGE_PROXY_BASE_URL` is empty, so the URL goes directly to Supabase's render endpoint. This might return different dimensions than the original endpoint.

But more importantly, looking at the screenshots, the issue is clear: portrait photos make cards extremely tall. This is a UX problem that needs fixing regardless of whether it existed before our changes. The key question is what height the card images should be - looking at the screenshots, around 200-250px would show the pet face nicely while keeping cards compact. I don't need to ask the user though - I can just create the fix. The solution is straightforward: add `object-fit: cover` and a fixed height or aspect-ratio to `.pet-image`, and optionally add a `height` parameter to the Image Transformation to save bandwidth.</details></think>

---

Ja tenho todas as informacoes necessarias para criar o plano. O problema esta claro:

**Diagnostico**: A classe CSS `.pet-image` possui `width: 100%; height: auto;`, o que faz com que as fotos em formato retrato (portrait) se expandam verticalmente sem limite. Como muitas fotos de pets sao tiradas com celular em modo retrato (proporcao ~2:3 ou 3:4), os cards ficam extremamente altos. Alem disso, a funcao `buildCardImageUrl` no `app.js` aplica apenas `width=400` na transformacao de imagem, sem restringir a altura, entao o Supabase retorna a imagem com o aspect ratio original preservado.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "plan": "# Corrigir altura dos cards de pets no dashboard\n\n## Diagnostico\n\nAs imagens dos pets sao exibidas com &#96;width: 100%; height: auto;&#96; no CSS (&#96;styles.css:230-241&#96;), sem nenhuma restricao de altura. Como muitas fotos sao em formato retrato (celular), o card se expande verticalmente ocupando quase toda a viewport.\n\nA funcao &#96;buildCardImageUrl&#96; em [&#96;app.js&#96;](c:\\Users\\femat\\Projetos Python\\Vi.Ca_Project\\Vi.Ca_App\\dash_public\\app.js) (linhas 41-59) envia apenas &#96;width=400&amp;quality=75&#96; para o endpoint de Image Transformation do Supabase, sem &#96;height&#96; nem &#96;resize&#96;, entao a imagem retornada preserva o aspect ratio original (ex: 400x600 para fotos retrato).\n\n## Correcoes\n\n### 1. CSS - Restringir altura da imagem no card\n\nEm [&#96;styles.css&#96;](c:\\Users\\femat\\Projetos Python\\Vi.Ca_Project\\Vi.Ca_App\\dash_public\\styles.css) (linhas 230-241), alterar &#96;.pet-image&#96;:\n\n**Antes:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;css\n.pet-image {\n    width: 100%;\n    height: auto;\n    display: block;\n    /* ... protecao contra copia ... */\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Depois:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;css\n.pet-image {\n    width: 100%;\n    aspect-ratio: 4 / 3;\n    object-fit: cover;\n    display: block;\n    /* ... protecao contra copia ... */\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- &#96;aspect-ratio: 4 / 3&#96; garante proporcao uniforme para todos os cards, independente da orientacao da foto original\n- &#96;object-fit: cover&#96; preenche a area sem distorcer, cortando as bordas quando necessario (centraliza automaticamente)\n- Isso e responsivo: adapta-se ao tamanho da coluna no grid\n\n### 2. JavaScript - Adicionar height e resize na transformacao de imagem\n\nEm [&#96;app.js&#96;](c:\\Users\\femat\\Projetos Python\\Vi.Ca_Project\\Vi.Ca_App\\dash_public\\app.js), adicionar constante &#96;IMAGE_TRANSFORM_HEIGHT&#96; e parametros &#96;height&#96; + &#96;resize=cover&#96; na funcao &#96;buildCardImageUrl&#96;:\n\n**Constantes (linha ~24):**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;javascript\nconst IMAGE_TRANSFORM_WIDTH = 400;\nconst IMAGE_TRANSFORM_HEIGHT = 300;\nconst IMAGE_TRANSFORM_QUALITY = 75;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Funcao &#96;buildCardImageUrl&#96; (linhas 49-53):**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;javascript\nif (parsed.pathname.includes(publicPath)) {\n    parsed.pathname = parsed.pathname.replace(publicPath, renderPath);\n    parsed.searchParams.set('width', String(IMAGE_TRANSFORM_WIDTH));\n    parsed.searchParams.set('height', String(IMAGE_TRANSFORM_HEIGHT));\n    parsed.searchParams.set('resize', 'cover');\n    parsed.searchParams.set('quality', String(IMAGE_TRANSFORM_QUALITY));\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIsso faz o Supabase entregar a imagem ja cropada em 400x300 (4:3), o que:\n- Reduz ainda mais o trafego de egress (imagem menor em bytes)\n- Garante que o CSS recebe uma imagem no formato correto, sem precisar cortar no client\n\n### 3. Rebuild do projeto\n\nRodar &#96;npm run build&#96; para gerar os arquivos em &#96;dist/&#96;.\n\n### Resultado esperado\n\n- Cards com altura uniforme e compacta (~75% da largura)\n- Fotos centralizam automaticamente mostrando a porcao mais relevante\n- Reducao adicional de egress no Supabase (imagens menores)\n- Modal de zoom continua usando a foto original em full resolution (sem alteracao)\n",
  "name": "Fix pet card height",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Alterar .pet-image em styles.css: substituir height:auto por aspect-ratio:4/3 + object-fit:cover",
      "status": "pending",
      "id": "css-aspect-ratio"
    },
    {
      "content": "Adicionar IMAGE_TRANSFORM_HEIGHT=300, parametro height e resize=cover em buildCardImageUrl no app.js",
      "status": "pending",
      "id": "js-height-param"
    },
    {
      "content": "Rodar npm run build e validar visualmente no browser",
      "status": "pending",
      "id": "rebuild"
    }
  ],
  "overview": "Corrigir a altura excessiva dos cards de pets no dashboard, adicionando restricao de aspect-ratio nas imagens via CSS e otimizando a transformacao de imagem no Supabase para entregar thumbnails ja cortados no formato correto."
}
```
Additional data:

```json
{
  "reviewData": {
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

---

Corrigi a altura dos cards conforme o plano, sem editar o arquivo de plano, e concluí todos os to-dos em sequência (`in_progress` → `completed`).

### O que foi alterado

- Em `styles.css`:
  - `.pet-image` mudou de `height: auto` para:
    - `aspect-ratio: 4 / 3`
    - `object-fit: cover`
- Em `app.js`:
  - adicionei `const IMAGE_TRANSFORM_HEIGHT = 300;`
  - em `buildCardImageUrl(...)`, passei também:
    - `height=300`
    - `resize=cover`
  - mantendo `width=400` e `quality=75`

### Verificação

- Rodei `npm run build` com sucesso.
- Validei no browser (`http://127.0.0.1:5500/index.html`):
  - os cards agora estão com altura uniforme e compacta;
  - as imagens não esticam mais verticalmente;
  - o layout ficou consistente entre colunas.
- `ReadLints` sem erros para `app.js` e `styles.css`.

Se quiser, no próximo passo eu também ajusto um breakpoint específico para mobile (ex.: usar `aspect-ratio: 1 / 1` abaixo de 768px) para deixar os cards ainda mais equilibrados em telas pequenas.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Explaining Netlify redirect fix and redeploy**

The missing `/supabase-img` rule in `_redirects` caused Netlify’s SPA fallback to intercept those requests, breaking image loading. Adding the proxy rule to `_redirects` before the fallback fixes this, but the user must redeploy for it to take effect. After redeploy, opening an image URL should return HTTP 200 serving the image. Clearing browser cache may help, and if problems persist, checking Netlify redirect settings is advised. Note that `_redirects` in `dist/` overrides `netlify.toml` rules during deployment.</details></think>

---

Corrigi a causa do problema: o Netlify estava servindo **`/index.html`** para as URLs de thumbnail porque o arquivo `_redirects` só tinha o fallback SPA. Isso fazia o `/supabase-img/*` cair no `/* -> /index.html` e a imagem quebrar.

O que fiz:
- Adicionei a regra de proxy no `_redirects`, **antes** do fallback SPA.
- Rodei `npm run build` para atualizar `dist/_redirects`.

Arquivo alterado:
- `_redirects`

Agora você precisa **redeployar** no Netlify para a regra nova entrar em produção.

Teste rápido após o deploy:
- Abra a URL da thumbnail quebrada e veja se volta a imagem:
  `https://adocoes-tempo-real.adotevica.com.br/supabase-img/storage/v1/render/image/public/...`
- Se o retorno não for mais HTML, o problema está resolvido.

Se quiser, posso também ajustar o `build.js` para garantir que qualquer mudança futura no proxy fique sempre sincronizada com o `_redirects`.
